user_query:
I’m sending a firmwide note encouraging associates to schedule PTO and prioritize mental health, and I want it grounded in our policy and a quick snapshot of current PTO usage. Please do the following using the files in this workspace and produce the specified outputs:

1) Run the provided script to generate the PTO summary:
- Execute: python tools/pto_summary.py
- This must read input/pto_snapshot.csv and write output/pto_summary.json. Create the output directory if it doesn’t exist.
- Do not compute the metrics by hand; use the JSON produced by the script.

2) Read and extract policy details to include in the email:
- From input/firm_vacation_policy.md, incorporate exactly these three facts into the email as a concise bullet list:
  • The annual PTO allotment for associates (in hours).
  • The rule for up to 3 consecutive business days off without partner pre-approval (and the notice requirement).
  • The recommendation for at least one uninterrupted 5-day period off per year.
- Do not invent or alter the values; use what appears in the policy file.

3) Apply tone and phrasing requirements:
- Use input/tone_guide.md to guide the rewrite. The final email must include at least two “Preferred phrases” from the guide and must not contain any “Avoid phrases.”

4) Edit the draft into a final email:
- Start from input/email_draft.txt and rewrite it into output/wellness_email.txt.
- Keep the original Subject line and the closing signature from the draft, but rewrite the body to reflect the supportive tone and include the policy bullet list and a short encouragement to schedule time off.
- Include a “By the numbers” section that uses values from output/pto_summary.json and lists exactly these four lines in this order (values must match the JSON):
  - Associates: <total_associates>
  - Avg PTO used (hours): <avg_pto_used_hours>
  - Median PTO used (hours): <median_pto_used_hours>
  - Associates with future days booked (%): <percent_with_future_days_booked>%

Deliverables:
- output/pto_summary.json (generated by the script)
- output/wellness_email.txt (final email as described)

Please ensure the final email uses first-person plural (“we/our”), includes at least two preferred phrases from the tone guide, contains none of the avoid phrases, and that the four metrics exactly match output/pto_summary.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
